original_hypothesis.title: Watchers.verifyRemoveLiquidity Is Empty, Providing No Withdrawal Verification
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Watchers.verifyRemoveLiquidity is an empty function that performs no validation. BaseConnector.sendTokensToTrustedAddress relies on this function to verify withdrawal amounts when the accounting manager requests tokens. Without verification, a compromised or malicious keeper can extract arbitrary amounts from connectors without any watcher-level oversight.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The Watchers contract is documented as a security monitoring role to undo suspicious actions. The empty verification function completely negates this security layer for token withdrawals from connectors to the accounting manager.

proof_of_signal.pattern: function verifyRemoveLiquidity(uint256 withdrawAmount, uint256 sentAmount, bytes memory data) external view { } — completely empty function body, no validation whatsoever

preconditions: Keeper/manager is compromised or acts maliciously

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Arbitrary token amounts can be extracted from connectors with no watcher validation
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires compromised keeper to exploit; designed security layer is missing

operational_questions: Was this intentionally left as a placeholder for future implementation?
Are there other mechanisms compensating for this missing verification?
Is the Watcher multisig threshold sufficient to prevent single-signer abuse?
validation_requirements: Verify that verifyRemoveLiquidity never reverts regardless of inputs
Test extraction of amounts exceeding legitimate withdrawal needs

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Compromise keeper keys (or collude with keeper signers)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Connector's sendTokensToTrustedAddress calls verifyRemoveLiquidity — passes trivially
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Tokens extracted to accounting manager, then rescued via emergency or routed elsewhere

potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Depends on detection speed; assets may already be moved
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All token withdrawals from connectors to accounting manager

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Unusually large token transfers from connectors
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: retrieveTokensForWithdraw amounts exceeding withdrawal group needs
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Token extraction without corresponding withdrawal execution

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
